title: cathartolinum tenellum
intro: Cathartolinum tenellum, also known as the small-seeded hollyhock, is a plant species in the mallow family that contains anthraquinone glycosides. These anthraquinone glycosides possess medicinal properties, acting as a laxative and having purgative effects. The health benefits of Cathartolinum tenellum include the relief of constipation, as well as the potential treatment of hemorrhoids and certain gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Cathartolinum tenellum can be found in the form of dried roots or leaves, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules for medicinal use.
